title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A duck boat overturned on a ramp at 10 Museum Way, injuring 11 people, including one seriously, in Cambridge.
The incident occurred after a tow rope snapped while the duck boat was being pulled from the Charles River.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations for the day and are collaborating with authorities to investigate the accident.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins acquired JJ Peterka for two first-round picks, aiming to bolster their offensive lineup.
Peterka, 24, averaged 26.6 goals over the past three seasons and could enhance Boston's scoring options.
Despite concerns over draft pick cost, Peterka's speed and offensive potential fit Bruins' needs for more dynamic play.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth will stop covering GLP-1 and other weight-loss drugs for obesity on July 1, affecting 22,000 members.
The policy change, part of Gov. Maura Healey’s fiscal 2027 budget, is expected to save $15 million annually.
MassHealth will continue coverage for these drugs if prescribed for other conditions like Type 2 diabetes.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins drafted two goalies, Yuri Ivanov and Roberto Henriquez, during Rounds 2-7 of the 2026 NHL Draft.
Boston selected wingers Nils Bartholdsson, Matvei Kotkov, and Oscar Olsson, emphasizing speed and skill development.
Defensemen Jacob Vandeven and Cullen McCrate were added for their physical presence and offensive potential.

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Two earthquakes in Venezuela killed 1,430 people and left 68,900 missing, AP reports.
Red Sox players, including Willson Contreras, wore 'VZ' hats to honor earthquake victims.
David Ortiz raised funds at Fenway Park for Venezuelan earthquake relief efforts.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized analytics suggesting he would be the seventh-best player on a team, defending his performance on social media.
NBA insider Bobby Marks reported mixed analytics views on Brown, sparking online debate about his value as a trade target.
Despite the analytics criticism, Brown is widely seen as a valuable player, with strong defenses from personalities like Stephen A. Smith.

title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox beat the New York Yankees 4-1, winning their third straight game against the A.L.-leading team.
Red Sox starter Jake Bennett held the Yankees hitless until the fifth, earning a win with 6.1 innings pitched.
Gerrit Cole allowed four runs on seven hits, raising his career ERA against Boston above six in a 5.1-inning outing.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins trade 2021 first-round pick Fabian Lysell to Colorado Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan.
Ivan, 23, played 66 games with the AHL's Colorado Eagles, scoring 11 goals and 15 assists last season.
Lysell only appeared in 12 NHL games, scoring one goal and two assists during his time with the Bruins.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in a World Cup match held at Boston Stadium, showcasing their attacking prowess.
French player Ousmane Dembélé scored one of the fastest hat-tricks in World Cup history during the match.
France is considered a favorite to win the World Cup, led by stars like Kylian Mbappe and Dembélé.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Cape Cod authorities investigate specific threat by student at Sandwich Middle High; police and DA's office involved.
Threat detected via safety app GoGuardian on a school-issued device, prompting police and superintendent responses.
Suspended student won't return to school; families of alleged targets have been notified by school officials.
